items.title: People have been using playoff losses to craft kneejerk narratives about star players since long before social media. Or the internet.
items.author: Mike Finger
items.body_excerpt:
People have been using playoff losses to craft kneejerk narratives about star players since long before social media. Or the internet.

I’m old enough — really, I am! — to remember when Michael Jordan was a great scorer who couldn’t win when it counted.

items.title: Wemby’s too soft. Mitch Johnson’s in over his head. Can’t win a title with Fox. It takes just a few rough June minutes to turn those narratives into basketball gospel.
items.author: Mike Finger

Wemby’s too soft. Mitch Johnson’s in over his head. Can’t win a title with Fox. It takes just a few rough June minutes to turn those narratives into basketball gospel.

The Spurs might spend years trying to prove them wrong.

Or they can start Saturday.

items.title: It’s just supply and demand, man. The organization isn’t setting those prices. The secondary market is.
items.author: Mike Finger
items.body_excerpt:
It’s just supply and demand, man. The organization isn’t setting those prices. The secondary market is. 

And if Knicks fans are willing to offer Spurs fans that much money, I’m certainly not going to lecture people about accepting an offer that I probably wouldn’t turn down, either.
